visuals.title: Lecture Note: Active RLC Derivation
visuals.caption: The note derives the transfer function of the active RLC circuit from an s-domain KCL equation.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Pole Regions as A Changes

images.caption: The note begins the pole and discriminant analysis.
The special boundary case A = 1 gives purely imaginary poles.
The full stable and unstable parameter regions are sketched on the A-axis.

visuals.title: The Boundary Point at A = 1
visuals.description: At A = 1 the poles are purely imaginary, so the system sits exactly on the stability boundary.

visuals.title: Stability Classification as A Changes
visuals.description: The circuit is stable for A < 1, marginal at A = 1, and unstable for A > 1.
